title: François Villeroy de Galhau: Little time left to wake up - can we reconcile Europe with speed?
summary: This is the final speech by François Villeroy de Galhau as Governor of the Banque de France, delivered at the 2026 Eurofi High Level Seminar on March 27, 2026. The extracted content consists only of introductory framing remarks, expressing concern about European urgency and competitiveness, but does not contain specific policy content. The full 10-page speech is available as a PDF but was not extracted for analysis.
key_points: Speech delivered virtually by Governor François Villeroy de Galhau at Eurofi High Level Seminar in Nicosia, Cyprus on March 27, 2026
Marked as his last speech as Governor of the Banque de France after 18 Eurofi meetings
Opening remarks frame the central question as 'How fast?' rather than 'What?' needs to be done for Europe
Extracted text contains no specific policy proposals, data, or numerical targets
Personal views stated explicitly: 'The views expressed in this speech are those of the speaker and do not necessarily reflect those of the BIS'
why_it_matters: The speech signals a closing perspective from a departing central bank governor on European policy urgency. Without the full text, the specific policy direction (hawkish or dovish) and concrete proposals cannot be determined. Full speech PDF (893kb, 10 pages) available but not included in extracted content.
macro_context: The speech title suggests focus on European competitiveness and the pace of reforms, likely touching on macro policy challenges facing the eurozone. Specific content cannot be verified from extracted text alone.
